Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 1 to 3 appear to be baseline idle state or initial startup They have low temperatures 20 to 21 pressure remains at 1 bar vibration is 0 material flow 0 tool position 0 mm energy usage around 0 point 50 kWh So these events are likely Idle or Preparation stage

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 4 to 6 are a sequence that could be interpreted as Cutting Metal because the temperature jump and vibration increase might represent machine tool operations like cutting machining etc The increase in temperature and vibration is consistent with friction and mechanical cutting operations

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Given the provided candidate list includes Cutting Metal I will assign this group as Cutting Metal Rationale The increase in temperature and vibration is consistent with friction and mechanical cutting operations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 7 to 8 have different pattern Temperature drops to 40 vibration decreases to 10 material flow drops to 2 pressure increases to 2 bar tool position jumps to 15 mm energy stays at 2 point 00 kWh

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: This might represent a transitional phase between cutting and another operation It could be Assembling Parts activity if the machine is now positioning parts for further assembly after cutting

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 9 is quite distinct Temperature jumps to 80 vibration spikes to 50 material flow drops to 0 tool position stays at 15 and energy consumption jumps to 5 point 00 kWh

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That pattern could be interpreted as Welding because welding operations often require high temperature and energy usage while there is no movement of raw material feed because it is stationary So I will assign event 9 as Welding

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 10 to 12 show Temperature decreases to around 30 vibration is very low 1 then 5 material flow increases from 0 to 1 tool position moves from 15 to 20 pressure goes back to 1 bar energy consumption decreases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: This group shows a cooling down phase with low energy and slight movement in tool position as if aligning or packaging the finished product

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Between Quality Inspection and Packaging Quality inspection normally involves sensor based measurements and might not be associated with material flow Packaging often involves a gentle movement of product into a container which can show slight changes in tool position and material flow So I will label group 5 as Packaging

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 13 to 15 indicate the machine returning to idle or baseline state after completing a cycle Temperature is around 20 to 21 pressure at 1 vibration 0 material flow 0 tool position resets back to 0 mm energy is low

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Therefore final process sequence Group 1 Idle Preparation Group 2 Cutting Metal Group 3 Assembling Parts Group 4 Welding Group 5 Packaging Group 6 Idle state
